skill.name: system-integration-validator
skill.description: Guides pre-deployment integration validation for ports, local databases, API contracts, and data-flow risks.
skill.summary: Validate integration readiness before deployment with focused checks for ports, databases, APIs, and workflow flow.
skill.author: BarisSozen

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography, local command examples, network examples, and filesystem redirection. Review found no cryptographic implementation, prompt injection, credential access, or exfiltration, but the skill does guide agents or users to run local diagnostic commands and inspect services.

medium_findings.title: Local Diagnostic Command Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill includes shell examples for checking open ports and local PostgreSQL and Redis availability. These commands are consistent with the stated integration-validation purpose, but they can reveal local service information if executed by an agent without user review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicitly present and perform local diagnostics. The risk is moderate because there is no automatic execution, no user-controlled shell construction beyond a fixed port loop, and no external destination.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged locations contain marketplace prose and skill descriptions, not calls to MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic algorithms. No evidence found for cryptographic implementation or insecure hashing behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are descriptive text and generated marketplace content. Searches found no weak crypto algorithm names or cryptographic API usage in the reported files.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Static Finding Is Benign Context
low_findings.description: The flagged audit_report.json line describes a best practice about checking ports before starting services. It is deployment guidance, not hidden reconnaissance logic or automated collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is plain documentation in a generated audit report. The actual skill also discusses port checks, but only as visible local diagnostic guidance.

low_findings.title: Generic Fetch Examples Require Review Before Use
low_findings.description: The skill shows a generic fetch call and a safer timeout version using an unspecified URL. There is no hardcoded endpoint or credential transfer, but users should verify URLs before execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The fetch calls are real examples but appear in a timeout teaching block. Risk remains low because there is no destination, payload, secret access, or automatic execution.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Redirection Is Limited To Suppressing Command Output
low_findings.description: The /dev/null reference suppresses output from a local port check. It does not read sensitive files, write persistent data, or traverse the filesystem.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only filesystem pattern is standard output redirection to /dev/null. This is a common shell idiom and does not create a meaningful filesystem access risk by itself.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill provides shell commands for local service checks. Require user confirmation before running them in an agent with terminal access.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell examples are present and executable if copied or run by an assistant. They are limited to local diagnostics and do not show malicious behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Request Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes generic fetch examples for timeout handling. The examples should not be executed against untrusted URLs without explicit user review.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network API calls are visible in an example block. No exfiltration target, payload, or credential source is present.

content.value_statement: Integration failures can delay releases and create avoidable production incidents. This skill gives structured checks for ports, databases, API contracts, and data-flow blockers.
actual_capabilities: Guides checks for common local service ports before deployment.
Provides validation steps for PostgreSQL and Redis connectivity.
Helps compare frontend API calls with backend endpoint behavior.
Identifies dead ends, orphan inputs, bottlenecks, and circular dependencies in workflows.
Recommends timeout handling for network operations that could otherwise hang.
limitations: It does not run tests unless the user or agent executes the suggested commands.
It does not discover every service port used by a custom environment.
It cannot prove production readiness without real environment access.
It does not replace full integration, contract, or load testing.

use_cases.title: Prepare a release checklist
use_cases.description: Build a focused validation flow for ports, local services, databases, and API readiness before deployment.

use_cases.title: Debug blocked service startup
use_cases.description: Trace conflicts around local ports, database availability, and dependent services when an application will not start.

use_cases.title: Review frontend-backend alignment
use_cases.description: Check whether frontend calls, backend routes, expected responses, and timeout handling match before release.

prompt_templates.title: Run a basic readiness review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the system-integration-validator skill to create a basic pre-deployment checklist for my local app.

prompt_templates.title: Check local service dependencies
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the system-integration-validator skill to help me check required ports, PostgreSQL, and Redis before I deploy.

prompt_templates.title: Validate API contracts
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the system-integration-validator skill to compare my frontend API calls with backend endpoints and response expectations.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze workflow failure paths
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the system-integration-validator skill to find dead ends, orphan inputs, bottlenecks, and circular dependencies in this workflow.

output: Ports: check whether application, API, database, cache, and proxy ports are available.
Databases: confirm PostgreSQL accepts connections and Redis responds to health checks.
API contract: compare frontend calls with backend routes, methods, and response shapes.
Data flow: review inputs, outputs, bottlenecks, and circular paths before release.

output_examples.input: My workflow gets stuck after the API call.
output: Map the request path from frontend action to backend handler and database operation.
Check for missing consumers, missing inputs, retry loops, and operations without timeouts.
Verify service health before changing application logic.

output_examples.input: Help me review integration risk for staging.
output: Start with environment dependencies, then review contracts, then inspect data flow.
Flag any port conflict, database failure, endpoint mismatch, or unbounded network operation.
Document remaining checks that require staging credentials or live service access.

best_practices: Review commands before execution and confirm they target the intended local environment.
Validate service health before debugging application code or changing deployment settings.
Use timeouts and clear failure paths for every network or service dependency.
anti_patterns: Running diagnostic commands in production without approval or change-control context.
Treating a successful port check as proof that the full service is healthy.
Skipping API contract review because frontend and backend builds both pass.

faq.question: Does this skill execute checks automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides steps and examples. A user or agent must decide whether to run any command.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: It is listed for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What systems does it focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on local ports, PostgreSQL, Redis, frontend-backend APIs, and workflow data flow.

faq.question: Can it replace integration tests?
faq.answer: No. It complements integration tests by checking environment and dependency readiness first.

faq.question: Is network access required?
faq.answer: Not for reading the skill. Some validation steps may involve local or configured service requests.

faq.question: What security review result applies?
faq.answer: The skill has medium risk because it includes local command and network examples, but no malicious behavior was confirmed.
